name: Terry Trotter
abstract: Terry William Trotter (born October 5, 1940) is an American jazz pianist and piano teacher living in Los Angeles. He has recorded with such artists as Frank Sinatra, Ella Fitzgerald, Natalie Cole, Celine Dion, Larry Carlton, and many others.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Sondheim and Jazz - Side by Side
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: And we became friends. We used to play four-hand classical music at his Steinway.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: I first met Bobby Hutcherson when Terry Trotter, the pianist, took me over to Bobby’s house to play with Bobby and him and Herbie Lewis the bass player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: So me and Herbie, we started hanging out with Terry... We used to go over to his house and sit there and listen to records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
